description: Shared type definitions for dev.cocore.compute.* records.

money.description: An amount of value, in integer minor units of a named currency. No floats. ISO 4217 codes are preferred; uppercase three-or-more-letter codes are accepted for non-fiat (e.g. XBT, XSAT, USDC).

amount.description: Integer minor units (e.g. cents for USD).

tokenCounts.description: Token counts associated with a unit of work.

modelPrice.description: A provider's offered price for a single model.

inputPricePerMTok.description: Integer minor units per 1,000,000 input tokens.

outputPricePerMTok.description: Integer minor units per 1,000,000 output tokens.

tokenRate.description: A model-agnostic per-token rate. Same shape as modelPrice without modelId. Used by an exchange to express a uniform rate that applies across providers/models. A receipt's price.amount is auditable against this rate as roughly `inputPricePerMTok * tokens.in / 1e6 + outputPricePerMTok * tokens.out / 1e6`, modulo a small floor for very short jobs.

inputPricePerMTok.description: Integer minor units per 1,000,000 input tokens.

outputPricePerMTok.description: Integer minor units per 1,000,000 output tokens.

trustLevel.description: How strongly the provider's environment is attested. `hardware-attested` means a verified Apple attestation (MDA chain or App Attest object) binds a genuine Apple device to the signing key; `self-attested` is a software key with no such chain. NOTE: `hardware-attested` alone does NOT imply the signing key is non-exportable — an MDA chain bound via freshness code attests the device that vouched for the PUBLIC key, not that the PRIVATE key is Secure-Enclave-resident. Key non-exportability is required only for the `attested-confidential` tier (see #tier) and is proven only by App Attest.

tier.description: Confidentiality tier of a unit of work — whether the prompt was provably handled only inside a measured, signed binary the owner cannot read. Distinct from `trustLevel` (which describes how the environment is attested) because a hardware-attested machine can still run a best-effort engine. A verifier MUST recompute this from evidence and never trust a self-asserted value. `attested-confidential` REQUIRES, in addition to a verified Apple attestation bound to the signing key: (1) a signing key proven Secure-Enclave-RESIDENT and non-exportable via a bound Apple App Attest object — an MDA-freshness binding is INSUFFICIENT because it does not prove the private key cannot leave the host, so a software key with a genuine MDA chain is portable to any machine (the 2026-07-05 spoof; see ADR-0003); (2) a known-good measured `cdHash`; (3) the hardened-runtime posture booleans (sip && hardenedRuntime && libraryValidation && !getTaskAllow && secureBoot); and (4) a fresh enclave-signed session key. Anything weaker is `best-effort`. Absent is equivalent to `best-effort` for pre-2026-06 readers.

settlementStatus.description: Lifecycle state of a settlement record.
